question: Which hypervisor type runs directly on bare metal with no host OS?
explanation: Type 1 (bare metal) hypervisors like VMware ESXi run without a host OS underneath — used in production data centers.

question: What is a VM snapshot?
choices: A full independent backup of the VM
A point-in-time saved state you can roll back to, usually stored as diffs
A screenshot of the VM's console
A clone of the VM on separate hardware

question: Which cloud service model has you managing only your application, with the provider handling everything underneath?
Which cloud service model has you managing the OS and up, while the provider manages raw compute/storage/network?
A company runs some workloads on AWS and some on its own private data center, moving load between them. What deployment model is this?
Under the cloud shared responsibility model, who is responsible for securing the customer's own data and access configuration?
What is VDI primarily used for?
choices: Running a centrally hosted desktop OS that users connect to remotely
